description:
The file '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/WorldHappiness.csv' is already open in JASP. This is real data from the World Happiness Report covering 155 countries with columns including Happiness Score, GDP per Capita, Family, Life Expectancy, Freedom, Generosity, and Government Corruption.

Conduct a complete regression analysis to determine what national-level factors predict happiness and whether GDP has a curvilinear (diminishing returns) relationship with happiness. The analysis should include: descriptive statistics (mean, SD, min, max) with distribution plots for Happiness Score, GDP per Capita, Family, Life Expectancy, and Freedom; a correlation matrix of these same five variables with significance flags and a heatmap; a multiple regression predicting Happiness Score from GDP per Capita, Family, Life Expectancy, and Freedom with collinearity diagnostics (VIF/Tolerance), residual plots (Q-Q and Residuals vs. Predicted), and Part/Partial correlations (this is Model 1); and a second regression (Model 2) that adds a computed column 'GDP_squared' (GDP per Capita times itself) to test the curvilinear hypothesis.

Save the complete analysis as '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/happiness_regression_pipeline.jasp'. Write a text report to '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/regression_report.txt' containing: the Pearson r between GDP per Capita and Happiness Score, the R-squared for Model 1, the R-squared for Model 2, the highest VIF value from Model 1, the p-value for GDP_squared in Model 2, and a one-sentence conclusion stating which model fits better.
